question: Based on Figures 5.4-1 and 5.4-2, does the NuScale SG design provide adequate access for inspection and maintenance?

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: Each NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel contains two integral steam generators, which are part of the reactor coolant system and share the vessel’s pressure boundary. The steam generator system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the reactor pressure vessel itself forming the shell and pressure boundary for the steam generators. The design ensures separation between the primary and secondary sides, with specific components forming the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems in the NuScale Power Module, detailing the integration of the reactor, steam generators, pressurizer, safety valves, and associated piping within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV). It outlines the configuration and boundaries of the steam generator system, emphasizing the integral design of the steam generators within the RPV and the separation between primary and secondary sides.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section describes the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components in the NuScale reactor, specifying types of steel and nickel-based alloys used for corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments. It also references relevant material specifications from the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) and lists the applicable standards for each component.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases relevant to reactor component materials and safety requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s design life. It details the components included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, all designed to meet regulatory definitions and maintain pressure containment.

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale's compliance with overpressure protection requirements by referencing applicable ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) and Operation and Maintenance (OM) Code cases, as mandated by various sections of 10 CFR 50.55a and related Regulatory Guides. It details how code cases for design, fabrication, construction, preservice, and inservice inspections and testing are selected and verified to meet NRC-endorsed standards, with a consolidated list provided in Table 5.2-1.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It details the use of ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) standards for design, fabrication, and testing, specifying quality classifications for various system components and confirming adherence to the 2013 Edition of the ASME BPVC as required by regulatory guidance.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: Threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components use Alloy 718, chosen for its compatibility with borated water and resistance to stress corrosion cracking, and comply with ASME BPVC standards. Preservice and inservice inspection and testing of pressure-retaining components follow ASME BPVC Section XI and relevant regulatory requirements, with inspection programs updated to the latest approved code editions for each inspection interval.

nuscale_ch05_0010.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Materials
nuscale_ch05_0010.summary: The Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) materials, including welds, are selected and fabricated in accordance with ASME BPVC standards and meet the regulatory requirements of 10 CFR 50, including General Design Criteria (GDC) for quality, environmental compatibility, and fracture prevention. The materials for Class 1 components, such as the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and steam generators (SGs), are specified in Table 5.2-4, ensuring compliance with fracture toughness and quality assurance standards.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale Reactor Coolant System's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the main degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that NuScale's novel helical tube and support design do not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing and standard SG program guidelines are deemed sufficient, with wear identified as the most likely degradation mechanism and secondary side corrosion risks considered low.

edges.description: Section A provides a general overview of the components within the NuScale Power Module, including the steam generators, while Section B references specific sections where details about pressurizer materials and components (some of which are also mentioned in Section A) can be found.

Section A describes the components and design features of the NuScale steam generators, while Section B lists references to industry standards and guidelines that inform or support the design and maintenance requirements for those components.
Section B provides detailed material specifications for pressurizer components that are generally described as part of the reactor coolant system in Section A.

Section A describes the physical arrangement and components of the NuScale reactor coolant system, including the steam generators, while Section B lists references to industry standards and guidelines that inform or constrain the design and maintenance requirements for these components.

Section A references Section B by citing Section 5.2 for material descriptions of the RPV upper shell and head, which are relevant to the inspection and maintenance considerations discussed in the analysis question.
Section B describes the steam generators as part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary whose integrity is addressed in Section A, linking the SG design to the overall RCPB integrity requirements.
Section B describes the specific components and subsystems, including the steam generators (SGs), that are part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary whose integrity is addressed in Section A.
Section B references Section A by citing Section 5.2 for material descriptions of the RPV upper shell and upper head, which are part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary discussed in Section A.

Section A outlines regulatory compliance and code requirements for reactor coolant boundary integrity, which includes the steam generators discussed in Section B, thereby establishing the regulatory framework that Section B's design details must satisfy.

Section A references compliance with ASME and NRC regulatory requirements for overpressure protection, while Section B describes the physical design and components of the reactor coolant system, including the steam generators (SGs), thus Section A provides the regulatory framework that Section B's design must satisfy.
Section B references Section A by citing Section 5.2 for material descriptions of the RPV upper shell and upper head, establishing a direct cross-reference for component details.

Section A states that the NuScale SG design complies with ASME BPVC inspection requirements, while Section B describes the physical configuration of the steam generators, providing context for how inspection and maintenance access is achieved.

Section A states that the NuScale SG design complies with ASME BPVC inspection requirements, while Section B describes the physical configuration of the SGs within the reactor system, providing context for how inspection and maintenance access is achieved.

Section B references Section A by indicating that material descriptions for the pressurizer components are provided in Section 5.2, which details compliance with codes and code cases relevant to inspection and maintenance access.

Section A discusses the inspection and testing requirements for reactor coolant pressure boundary components, while Section B describes the physical configuration of the reactor pressure vessel and its internal components, including the steam generators, thus Section B provides the design context necessary to assess whether the inspection and maintenance requirements in Section A can be met for the SGs.

Section A specifies the material and inspection requirements for threaded fasteners used in the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which are components described in Section B's general overview of the reactor coolant system design, including the steam generators.

Section A describes the materials and inspection requirements for threaded fasteners in the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B provides detailed material specifications for pressurizer components, with both sections referencing each other to ensure material compatibility and compliance with inspection standards.

Section A describes the material and regulatory compliance of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B outlines the physical configuration and components within the reactor pressure vessel, including the steam generators, thus Section B provides the context in which the materials and requirements from Section A are applied.

Section A specifies the material and regulatory requirements for the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B describes the physical design and arrangement of reactor coolant system components, including the steam generators, thus Section A provides the foundational material standards that underpin the design features discussed in Section B.
Section B references Section A by citing Section 5.2 for descriptions of the materials used in the RPV upper shell and upper head, establishing a direct cross-reference for material specifications.

Section A outlines the design features required to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B details the compliance with ASME and NRC codes that govern the design, fabrication, and testing of those same boundary components, thus Section B provides the regulatory and code compliance framework for the design features described in Section A.

Section A describes the design features intended to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B specifies that these features comply with regulatory codes and standards (10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC) that include requirements for inspection and maintenance access.

Section A establishes the requirement to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B describes the inspection and testing provisions for threaded fasteners that are part of that boundary, thus providing specific implementation details for the general requirement.
Section B specifies the material requirements and standards that must be met to ensure the integrity described in Section A for the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

Section A references the use of ASME BPVC code cases for design and construction, while Section B establishes that the design complies with 10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC requirements, thus Section A provides specific details supporting the general compliance claim in Section B.

Section A references the use of ASME BPVC codes for design and construction, while Section B specifies that threaded fasteners used in the reactor coolant pressure boundary conform to these same ASME BPVC requirements, directly connecting the general code compliance in A to the specific material and inspection details in B.

Section A references the ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements that govern the design and construction of the reactor coolant boundary, while Section B specifies that the materials used for the reactor coolant pressure boundary comply with those same ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, thus providing detailed material conformance under the general regulatory framework described in Section A.

Section A establishes that the NuScale design complies with ASME BPVC requirements for inspection, while Section B specifies that threaded fasteners in the reactor coolant pressure boundary are selected and designed in accordance with those same ASME BPVC requirements.

Section A states that the design, fabrication, and inspection of the reactor coolant pressure boundary comply with ASME BPVC Section III, while Section B specifies that the materials used also conform to the same code requirements, thus Section B provides detailed material compliance supporting the general code compliance claim in Section A.

Section A specifies the use and compliance of threaded fasteners within the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B provides the general regulatory and code compliance requirements for all reactor coolant pressure boundary materials, including those fasteners.

Section A discusses observed degradation mechanisms and inspection program guidelines for SG tubing, while Section B provides a general description of the SG's integration and components, with both sections connected by their focus on SG design features relevant to inspection and maintenance access.

Section A discusses observed degradation mechanisms and inspection program applicability for SG tubing, while Section B provides a general description of the SG's integration and components, thus Section A provides specific operational and inspection considerations that specify details for the general SG design described in Section B.
